name: Focaccia Versiliese (Crispy Olive-&-Herb Filled Focaccia)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, stir the yeast into the warm water and let proof for 10 minutes.
2. Stir in the olive oil, rosemary, sage, olives, and garlic. Mix in the flours and salt with a wooden spoon until the dough is thick and smooth.
3. Knead the dough by hand for 8 to 10 minutes until it is firm and elastic.
4. Place the dough in a lightly oiled container, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise until doubled.
5. Turn the dough onto an oiled baking pan measuring 10 1/2 inches X 15 1/2 inches and stretch it to fit. If it won't fit, let it rest for 10 minutes and try again.
6. Cover with a towel and leave until it has risen half, about 30 minutes.
7. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) 30 minutes before baking.
8. Dimple the top of the dough with your fingertips and sprinkle with extra salt and 2 tablespoons olive oil just before baking.
9. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes until golden. Slide off the baking sheet onto a rack and let cool for a few minutes before eating warm or at room temperature.
Variation: Focaccia Dolce
Substitute 2 ounces raisins and 1 ounce pine nuts for the olives, herbs, and garlic. Soak the raisins in warm water for 30 minutes before using and toast the pine nuts.
